title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Massapequa Park, NY | $900-$2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Massapequa Park, Long Island. Safe, humane bat control for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today!
intro: Massapequa Park homeowners dealing with bats in their attics, chimneys, or eaves need fast, humane solutions. Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Massapequa Park and surrounding Long Island communities. Our licensed technicians safely remove bat colonies and seal entry points to prevent future infestations.
whyUs: With over a decade serving Long Island communities like Massapequa Park, we combine local expertise with proven bat exclusion techniques. Our team is fully licensed, insured, and trained in humane bat removal methods that comply with New York State wildlife regulations.
localContext: Massapequa Park's tree-lined streets and charming older homes near Marjorie R. Post Community Park provide ideal roosting spots for little brown bats and big brown bats. The area's proximity to Massapequa Creek and mature residential neighborhoods means seasonal bat activity is common, especially in attics and soffit areas of Cape Cod and Colonial-style homes.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Massapequa Park?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Massapequa Park typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your home's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: When is bat season in Massapequa Park, NY?
faqs.answer: Bats are most active in Massapequa Park from April through October. However, maternity season (May through August) requires special care, as New York law protects nursing mothers and their pups. We conduct exclusions before or after this period to ensure humane, legal removal.

faqs.question: Are bats in my attic dangerous?
faqs.answer: Yes, bats pose health risks through their droppings (guano), which can contain histoplasmosis spores, and potential rabies exposure. Bat guano also damages insulation and creates unpleasant odors. Professional removal is essential to protect your family's health and preserve your home's structure.

faqs.question: How do you prevent bats from returning to my Massapequa Park home?
faqs.answer: Our exclusion process involves identifying all entry points (often gaps as small as 3/8 inch), installing one-way exit devices, and permanently sealing access points with durable materials once all bats have left. We also provide attic cleanup and sanitization services to remove contaminated insulation and guano.
